revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Fujisaki Hachimangū]][[fr:Fujisaki Hachiman-gū]][[ja:藤崎八旛宮]] {{short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=struct|藤崎八幡宮|date=November 2010}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Fujisaki Hachiman-gū
藤崎八幡宮 | image = Fujisakihachimanguu-shrine.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=220}} | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|32|48|30|N|130|43|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark_source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}
[[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] | founded_by = | established = 935 | date_destroyed = | location = Igawabuchi-machi, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Kumamoto|en|Chūō-ku, Kumamoto|qid=Q137716|lt=Chūō-ku, Kumamoto|1=Chūō-ku, Kumamoto}}
{{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}} 860-0841 | website = {{URL|http://fujisakigu.or.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Fujisaki Hachiman-gū'''|藤崎八幡宮|}} is a [[Shinto]] {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Kumamoto|en|Chūō-ku, Kumamoto|qid=Q137716|lt=Chūō-ku, Kumamoto|1=Chūō-ku, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} and [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]. ==History== In 935, Fujisaki Hachimangu was established with the [[Bunrei]] of [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman-gū]] {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, at Chausuyama (now Kumamoto Fujisakidai Baseball Stadium), {{ill|Kumamoto Castle|en|Kumamoto Castle|qid=Q613355|lt=Kumamoto Castle|1=Kumamoto Castle}} at the order of {{ill|Emperor Suzaku|en|Emperor Suzaku|qid=Q361019|lt=Emperor Suzaku|1=Emperor Suzaku}}. The word Fuji derives from a tale that at the time of establishment, sticking of fuji resulted in fuji [[Wisteria]] taking root and grew. Fujisaki Hachimangu has been respected as the defender of Higo, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}. In 1542, {{ill|Emperor Go-Nara|en|Emperor Go-Nara|qid=Q349467|lt=Emperor Go-Nara|1=Emperor Go-Nara}} presented a wooden frame ''八幡藤崎宮'' which is now engraved over the [[Torii]]. Rebuilding of the shrine, 20 years apart, had been made with the order of the {{ill|Emperor|en|Emperor|qid=Q39018|lt=Emperor|1=Emperor}}. In 1877, the shrine was burnt amid the battle of {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|qid=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|1=Satsuma Rebellion}} and was reconstructed at Igawabuchi Machi, the present location. In the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]], Fujisaki was listed in 1915 among the 3rd class of nationally significant shrines or {{nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei-shōsha]]''|国幣小社}}. In 1952, the shrine was designated a [[religious corporation]]. ==Great Festival == {{main|Boshita Festival}} Of the events of the shrine, most known is the parade of [[Kami]] with horses in September. This had been called Boshita Festival because ''seko'' (followers) followed dancing horses, shouting, "Boshita, boshita." However, this reminded some people of the term "Horoboshita," a phrase or slogan which loosely meant "Korea is destroyed," and supposedly used during the [[:en:Imjin Wars|Imjin Wars]] of the 1590s, when {{ill|Katō Kiyomasa|en|Katō Kiyomasa|qid=Q1069843|lt=Katō Kiyomasa|1=Katō Kiyomasa}} was part of a campaign to conquer Korea. Now the festival attendants shout, "Doukai, doukai." ==Treasures== *A wooden sitting [[Hachiman]] statue and a female god statue are designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} . There are old documents, swords and other weapons. ==References== *Pamphlet of the Fujisaki Hachimanguu on Nov. 26, 2010. ==See also== {{Portal|Japan|Architecture}} {{commons Category|Fujisaki-hachimangu}} *[[:en:Katou Shrine|Katou Shrine]] *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}}{{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[en:Fujisaki Hachimangū]] {{Commons category|Fujisaki-hachimangu}} [[fr:Fujisaki Hachiman-gū]] [[ja:藤崎八旛宮]] {{wikidata link|Q167146}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumamoto]] [[Category:Culture articles needing translation from Japanese Wikipedia]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
